title: pyrus pashia var. obtusata
intro: Pyrus pashia var. obtusata, also known as the Himalayan apple or Chilgoza pine, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its edible seeds and medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pyrus pashia var. obtusata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into tea, infusion, or powder, and are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
